entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A hardy, widely adapted, drought tolerant legume crop grown in SAT regions.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand periods of water scarcity.

entities.name: SAT REGIONS
entities.description: Semi-arid tropical regions where pigeonpea is an integral component of cropping systems.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production per unit area, measured in kg ha-1 for pigeonpea.

entities.name: HETEROTIC POTENTIAL
entities.description: The inherent genetic advantage of hybrid offspring over their parents.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop where hybrid technology successfully increased yield.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal crop where hybrid technology successfully increased yield.

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop where hybrid technology successfully increased yield.

entities.name: GRAIN SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop where hybrid technology successfully increased yield.

entities.name: HYBRID TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The process of crossing genetically diverse parents to produce superior hybrid offspring.

entities.name: HAND EMASCULATION
entities.description: The manual removal of anthers from a flower to prevent self-pollination.

entities.name: POLLINATION
entities.description: The transfer of pollen from the male anther to the female stigma.

entities.name: MALE STERILE LINES
entities.description: Plant lines unable to produce functional pollen, used to facilitate hybrid seed production.

entities.name: NATURAL OUT-CROSSING
entities.description: The process of pollination between different individuals of the same species occurring naturally.

entities.name: ICPH 8
entities.description: The first pigeonpea hybrid developed using a GMS source in 1991.

entities.name: GMS SOURCE
entities.description: Genic Male Sterility source used for hybrid development.

entities.name: PPH4
entities.description: A pigeonpea hybrid variety.

entities.name: COH1
entities.description: A pigeonpea hybrid variety.

entities.name: AKPH4104
entities.description: A pigeonpea hybrid variety.

entities.name: AKPH2022
entities.description: A pigeonpea hybrid variety.

entities.name: GMS SYSTEM
entities.description: Genic Male Sterility system used in early pigeonpea hybrid development.

entities.name: CMS-BASED PIGEONPEA HYBRID
entities.description: Hybrids developed using Cytoplasmic Male Sterility.

entities.name: GTH-1
entities.description: The first CMS-based pigeonpea hybrid.

entities.name: ICPH 267
entities.description: The first commercial CMS hybrid pigeonpea with superior root characteristics.

entities.name: ROOT MASS
entities.description: The total amount of root material in a plant.

entities.name: ROOT DEPTH
entities.description: The vertical extent of the root system in the soil.

entities.name: MOISTURE DRAW
entities.description: The ability of roots to extract water from deeper soil profiles.

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The rate and extent of root development.

entities.name: EARLY SEASON DROUGHT
entities.description: Water scarcity occurring during the early stages of the crop growth cycle.

entities.name: RAINFED CROPS
entities.description: Crops that rely solely on rainfall for moisture and are not irrigated.

entities.name: SEED PRODUCTION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Standardized processes for the large-scale production of commercial hybrid seeds.

entities.name: SSR
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeats used as molecular markers for genetic analysis.

entities.name: HYBRID PURITY ASSESSMENT KITS
entities.description: Tools developed using SSR markers to test the genetic purity of hybrids.

entities.name: GOT
entities.description: Genotype or genetic purity of the hybrid seed.

entities.name: HYDRAULIC CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: The physical properties of roots that govern the movement of water and nutrients.

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The process by which plants absorb minerals from the soil.

entities.name: WATER UPTAKE
entities.description: The process by which plants absorb water from the soil.

entities.name: DRY LANDS
entities.description: Arid or semi-arid regions characterized by low rainfall.
